expectations.text: Output contains an explicit rating on a defined scale
expectations.evidence: Rating: 4 — Yes, with full 5-point scale table

expectations.text: Strengths section with specific evidence from the resume
expectations.evidence: 7 strengths, each grounded in specific resume items (Spotify promotion, 50k txn/day pipeline, Go rate-limiting library)

expectations.text: Gaps section distinguishing unknowns from red flags
expectations.evidence: 4 gaps, all explicitly labeled 'Unknown' — mentorship, mid-size org experience, API design for external consumers, PostgreSQL modeling depth

expectations.text: Risks section addresses title/level concern and startup-to-midsize transition
expectations.evidence: 4 risks: title sensitivity, startup-to-midsize pace, 'Staff at tiny startup' calibration, retention risk
Full decision framework: 'strong fit if...', 'not a fit if...', 'what would change your mind' — all specific and grounded
7 questions, all behavioral ('Tell me about a time...', 'Walk me through...'). Each has Why/Good looks like/Probe/Red flag structure

expectations.text: Includes a scoring rubric for the call
expectations.evidence: 5-dimension rubric (Mentorship, Architectural ownership, Cross-team influence, Adaptability, Motivation) with Strong/Acceptable/Weak signal columns

expectations.text: Questions target identified gaps, not generic
expectations.evidence: Q1 targets mentorship gap, Q2 targets consensus-building, Q3 targets title risk, Q5 targets data modeling gap, Q6 targets cross-team influence
